title: Mod descriptor (UI contract)
description: Machine-readable per-mod descriptor consumed by the mcp UI — the EM-safe-mod parity of the connector config descriptor (FX-CFG-001). Holds UI-rendering + disclosure data only: what evidence the mod CONSUMES, what advisory artifacts it EMITS, the EM-safe forbidden_actions boundary (the trust-disclosure surface), and enablement knobs. A mod has NO credentials and NO live network — it is an advisory post-processor over the neutral evidence stream (ADR-0008/0013). Structural rules live here; the semantic rules (id == folder; emits + forbidden_actions consistent with the enforced manifest.yaml; index/SETUP freshness) are enforced by scripts/validate_mod_config.py.

id.description: Stable mod id; equals the folder name AND the `run-mods` key (e.g. 'noisy_source_gate').

manifest_id.description: The mod's manifest.yaml `id` (hyphenated, e.g. 'noisy-source-gate'); cross-referenced by the validator.

description.description: One-line summary for the UI mod card.

icon.description: Optional icon/logo slug for the UI.

family.description: Mod family/category for grouping (e.g. evidence-contract, security, review-risk, routing, data-governance, trust, testing, freshness, dependency, webhook).

advises_on.description: Human summary of the risk/signal the mod detects and advises on.

consumes.description: The evidence-stream surface the mod READS (the input disclosure) — e.g. 'source_ref fields', 'changed file paths', 'evidence excerpt text', 'source_id'. A mod reads the neutral AdapterEmission, never a raw secret.

emits.description: The advisory artifact types the mod EMITS (must be consistent with the enforced manifest.yaml `outputs`) — e.g. routing_hint, source_evidence_annotation, advisory_governance_result, owner_lens_hint, suggested_review_question, dependency_signal.

non_authoritative.description: Always true — a mod is non-authoritative by construction (ADR-0008). The UI renders this as the 'advisory only, never decides' trust badge.

forbidden_actions.description: The actions the mod can NEVER take (must equal the enforced manifest.yaml `forbidden_actions`) — the UI's trust-disclosure surface, e.g. write_canonical_decision, approve_signoff, resolve_compliance, create_blocking_ci_result, bypass_governance_policy, mutate_source_evidence, collapse_confidence_score.

default_enabled.description: Whether the UI should show the mod enabled by default.

trust_gated.description: True when the mod's behavior is conditioned on an operator-raised source trust tier (e.g. noisy_source_gate) — the UI surfaces the trust knob.

config.description: Optional per-mod enablement knobs / nice-to-haves the UI renders (operator-runtime config; no secrets).

requirements.description: What the mod needs to run — for an EM-safe mod this is the neutral evidence stream + (optionally) the operator config knobs. NO credentials, NO live network.

advisory_only.description: Always true — the UI renders the 'advisory only' badge; a mod output is never a decision.

card_blurb.description: Optional short card subtitle for the UI.
